generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls vehicle actuation commands (steering, motor, servo) via ROS2 network for autonomous adjustments in adaptive cruise control systems"
]

instance AerospaceCollisionActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Executes real-time collision avoidance trajectory adjustments in aerospace systems based on digital twin feedback loops"
]

instance BatteryPowerController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Dynamic power distribution controller for battery systems using real-time digital twin feedback for optimal resource allocation"
]

instance ManufacturingSupplyChainController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Dynamic reconfiguration controller for supply chain systems in manufacturing using digital twin simulations for operational efficiency"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time vehicle state data (position, speed, acceleration) from on-board sensors via ROS2 network"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Acquires raw LiDAR point cloud data from vehicle sensors for environmental mapping and object tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointCloud
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures motor current and performance metrics via embedded systems for predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPerformanceMetrics
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks steering angle inputs for vehicle dynamics simulation and adaptive control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringInputData
]

instance ServoPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Records servo position data for vehicle chassis dynamics modeling"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPositionData
]

instance BatteryVoltageSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors battery voltage and health parameters using edge computing for real-time diagnostics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryHealthMetrics
]

instance AerospaceComponentWearSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks degradation patterns in aerospace components using historical data and AI models for failure prediction"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ComponentDegradationData
]

instance ManufacturingProcessSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time manufacturing process data via IIoT gateways for production line optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ProcessPerformanceData
]

instance ThermalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures thermal parameters in aerospace and battery systems for structural integrity monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ThermalData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance MotorPerformanceMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance SteeringInputData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
]

instance ServoPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoPositionSensor
]

instance BatteryHealthMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BatteryVoltageSensor
]

instance ComponentDegradationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AerospaceComponentWearSensor
]

instance ProcessPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingProcessSensor
]

instance ThermalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ThermalSensor
]

instance ProcessedLiDARClusters : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RawLiDARPointCloud
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleActuator
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance VehicleDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based vehicle dynamics simulator using MATLAB/Simulink for realistic modeling of vehicle behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance AerospaceCollisionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Spacecraft collision avoidance simulator using NASA STK for real-time risk modeling and trajectory adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ANSYSSimulation
]

instance ManufacturingProcessSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Production line optimization simulation adhering to ISO 23247-1 for defect prediction and resource allocation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KubernetesEdgeOrchestration
]

instance BatteryThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal/structural simulation of battery systems using ANSYS for lifecycle analysis and safety validation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EdgeComputingPlatform
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AI-driven predictive maintenance model using historical data and ML models for automotive system failure prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MLFlowModelVersioning
]

instance DemandForecastingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Demand forecasting model for automotive OTA updates and manufacturing inventory planning using AI/ML"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AWSDataLake
]

instance ClosedLoopControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time closed-loop control model implementing adaptive cruise control algorithms for autonomous vehicle adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

instance SemanticVisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic mapping model using ISO/IEC 30173 ontology for context-aware visualization of physical entities"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProtegeOntologyEditor
]

instance RealTimeDataArchive : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Centralized real-time data storage using Apache Kafka for streaming sensor data from edge devices"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData, RawLiDARPointCloud, MotorPerformanceMetrics
]

instance ProcessedDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical data repository using AWS S3 for predictive analytics and demand forecasting"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedLiDARClusters, BatteryHealthMetrics, ProcessPerformanceData
]

instance RelationshipGraphDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Graph database storing interdependencies between physical/digital entities using Neo4j for supply chain and system-of-twinned-systems integration"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessPerformanceData, ComponentDegradationData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Network : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network infrastructure for intra-system communication between vehicle sensors and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance WiFiUDPTransport : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi over UDP transport layer for inter-entity communication between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService, OptimizationService
]

instance EdgeComputingPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson/Intel Xeon edge devices for localized processing of high-frequency sensor data (battery systems)"
    DTDFVocab:enables BatteryThermalModel, PredictiveMaintenanceModel
]

instance OPCUAProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OPC Unified Architecture for data exchange between digital twins and Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)"
    DTDFVocab:enables ManufacturingProcessSimulation, ManufacturingSupplyChainController
]

instance KafkaStreamingDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Apache Kafka for real-time streaming data ingestion from edge devices to digital twin systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataArchive, DemandForecastingModel
]

instance InfluxDBTimeSeries : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB for low-latency time-series storage of sensor data for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionService
]

instance ProtegeOntologyEditor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Protege ontology editor for semantic modeling of digital twin concepts according to ISO/IEC 30173"
    DTDFVocab:enables SemanticVisualizationModel
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink platform for physics-based vehicle dynamics modeling and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleDynamicsModel, ClosedLoopControlModel
]

instance ANSYSSimulation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ANSYS tools for thermal/structural simulation of battery systems and aerospace components"
    DTDFVocab:enables BatteryThermalModel, AerospaceCollisionModel
]

instance MLFlowModelVersioning : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MLflow for automated model versioning and lifelong learning pipelines in digital twin evolution"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceModel, DemandForecastingModel
]

instance KubernetesEdgeOrchestration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kubernetes for managing edge workloads and ensuring scalability in distributed digital twin systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables ManufacturingProcessSimulation, ManufacturingSupplyChainController
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker for lightweight IoT messaging between sensors and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance AWSDataLake : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AWS S3 data lake for historical data storage and predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:enables DemandForecastingModel
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking service enabling continuous observation of physical parameters via IoT sensors and edge computing"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operational efficiency service using digital twin simulations to optimize production lines and vehicle routing"
    DTDFVocab:provides EfficiencyOptimizationAction, ResourceAllocationAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Failure prediction and demand forecasting service using AI/ML models for proactive maintenance and inventory planning"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, DemandForecastingInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control service enabling autonomous adjustments to physical systems via digital twin feedback loops"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutonomousAdjustmentAction, SafetyCriticalInterventionAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization service providing context-aware dashboards for physical system monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractiveDashboardAction, SemanticMappingInsight
]

instance CrossDomainCollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling integration of automotive, aerospace, and manufacturing twins for interoperable workflows"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemOfTwinnedSystemsAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous observation of physical parameters (vehicle performance metrics, battery health, aerospace component wear) via IoT sensors"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "AI/ML-based identification of deviations from expected behavior for predictive maintenance alerts"
]

instance EfficiencyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization recommendations for production lines and vehicle routing minimizing energy consumption and downtime"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ResourceAllocationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic reconfiguration of supply chains and power distribution based on real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Models degradation patterns in aerospace components and battery cells using historical data + AI to forecast failures"
]

instance DemandForecastingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Demand forecasting for automotive OTA updates and manufacturing inventory planning reducing inventory costs"
]

instance AutonomousAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous adjustments to physical systems (adaptive cruise control) via digital twin feedback loops"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyCriticalInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time collision avoidance interventions in aerospace systems triggering corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InteractiveDashboardAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-friendly interface for monitoring and control via 3D/4D visualizations of physical systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SemanticMappingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic mapping using ontologies to enable context-aware visualization linking abstract concepts to physical entities"
]

instance SystemOfTwinnedSystemsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Integration workflows enabling cross-domain collaboration between automotive, aerospace, and manufacturing twins"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance VehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehicleStateSensor, LiDARPointCloudSensor, MotorCurrentSensor,
        SteeringAngleSensor, ServoPositionSensor, VehicleActuator
]

instance AerospaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AerospaceComponentWearSensor, AerospaceCollisionActuator,
        ThermalSensor, BatteryVoltageSensor, AerospaceCollisionModel
]

instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ManufacturingProcessSensor, ManufacturingSupplyChainController,
        ManufacturingProcessSimulation, RelationshipGraphDatabase
]

instance BatteryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BatteryVoltageSensor, BatteryPowerController,
        BatteryThermalModel, EdgeComputingPlatform
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehicleDynamicsModel, PredictiveMaintenanceModel,
        DemandForecastingModel, ClosedLoopControlModel,
        SemanticVisualizationModel, ROS2Network, OPCUAProtocol
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Not in Document**
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin requires **time synchronization with 100 ms accuracy** between physical and digital components for computation offloading and safety-critical vehicular applications. This ensures precise coordination for real-time data processing, actuation, and sensor-actuator interactions. The temporal granularity aligns with automotive safety standards (e.g., ISO 23247), where strict synchronization is mandatory for reliable system operation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
  The Digital Twin system follows a **multi-entity architecture** based on ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA), structured into three primary entities:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts hardware-specific resources (e.g., sensors, actuators, compute) and general automotive functions like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*. Each entity is deployed on distinct hardware components, enabling decentralized resource management.
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data preprocessing, control mechanisms, and device communication, reflecting the system’s inherent capabilities.
  3. **User Entity**: Contains the *User Interface FE*, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components, ensuring user-facing interactivity.

  **Coordination mechanisms** are implemented via ROS2 packages (individual nodes per FE), enabling modular execution and entity-based ownership. Deviations from ISO 23247 RA (e.g., broader scope for the Physical Twin Entity) impact system engineering but align with hardware constraints."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard defines a lifecycle framework for Digital Twins in manufacturing, structured around an **entity-based reference architecture (RA)** where each lifecycle phase is governed by distinct **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)** and their associated **Digital Twin Entities (DT Entities)**. While the standard does not explicitly enumerate phases, the lifecycle implicitly follows a **development-to-operation continuum** with the following conceptual stages:

1. **Design & Conceptualization Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: Digital Twin entities are initially abstracted as **Functional Entities (FEs)** representing conceptual OMEs (e.g., equipment schematics, process flows).
   - **Transition Management**: Transition occurs via **data-driven validation** (e.g., simulation-based fidelity checks) before physical implementation.

2. **Development & Implementation Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: DTs adopt **real-time data streams** (e.g., from sensors) and **digital twins of OMEs** (e.g., CNC machines, assembly lines) with **OPC UA** (OPC Unified Architecture) for interoperability.
   - **Transition Management**: Governed by **ISO 23247 Part 4 (Information Exchange)** standards, ensuring seamless handoff between simulation and operational DTs.

3. **Operational Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: DTs dynamically mirror **live OMEs** via **continuous data collection** (e.g., IoT sensors) and **real-time control** (e.g., via Data Collection and Device Control Entities).
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Enforced by **version control** (e.g., Git-based repositories for FE updates) and **quality assurance** (e.g., ISO 9001-compliant testing protocols).

*Note*: The provided documents do not specify explicit phases or governance tools, but the architecture implies a **modular, entity-driven lifecycle** with **ISO 23247 Part 2** as the foundational framework.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 framework emphasizes **validation and verification** through the **Digital Twin Entity**, ensuring alignment with physical OMEs via real-time data synchronization. While the standard does not detail explicit fidelity requirements, **uncertainty quantification** is implied through the entity-based validation process (e.g., comparing digital and physical behavior). Safety constraints are implicitly addressed by the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, which enforces controlled interactions with OMEs. Quality assurance is achieved through iterative FE implementations, though no specific error detection/correction mechanisms or accuracy thresholds are defined.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Intra-Entity:** ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for software component communication.
    - **Inter-Entity:** Wi-Fi over UDP at the transport layer for entity-to-entity communication.

  - **Network Topology and Architecture:**
    - ROS2 operates within a local network (likely on-board or edge device).
    - Wi-Fi network connects entities (e.g., vehicle, digital twin) over a broader network (e.g., cloud/edge).

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - High-bandwidth requirements for real-time LiDAR data (point clouds, clustering/tracking).
    - Low-latency requirements for control feedback (e.g., actuation commands, vehicle state updates).
    - Not explicitly quantified, but implied to be critical for autonomous driving applications.

  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - ROS2 supports modularity and fault tolerance via distributed nodes.
    - Wi-Fi UDP may lack redundancy; potential reliance on hardware redundancy (e.g., dual LiDAR sensors) for critical data.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - Not explicitly detailed. Potential risks include:
      - Data integrity (e.g., tampering with sensor data).
      - Authentication (e.g., verifying digital twin commands).
      - Encryption for Wi-Fi communication (UDP may lack built-in security; likely requires custom middleware).
    - **Assumption:** Likely uses TLS/SSL for data in transit or hardware-level security (e.g., secure boot for vehicle components).

---

  **Key Limitation:** The document does not specify redundancy, encryption, or latency guarantees beyond general real-time requirements.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Deployment:** Primary deployment appears to be on-board the vehicle (e.g., LiDAR, sensors, ROS2 nodes).
    - **Cloud/Edge Hybrid:** Potential for cloud-based reporting, storage, or visualization (e.g., ACC services, data translation).
    - **On-Premise:** Not explicitly mentioned; likely not applicable for autonomous vehicles.

  - **Computational and Storage Requirements:**
    - **Computational:**
      - High-performance processing for LiDAR clustering/tracking (real-time processing of high-density point clouds).
      - ROS2 nodes require lightweight, deterministic execution (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Intel Xeon).
    - **Storage:**
      - Temporary storage for raw point clouds (e.g., 100+ MB/s for high-frequency LiDAR).
      - Long-term storage for reports/data collection (e.g., cloud-based databases like AWS S3 or on-board SSDs).

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - ROS2 supports horizontal scaling via distributed nodes (e.g., multiple LiDAR processing units).
    - Cloud-based reporting/storage can scale with user demand (e.g., ACC services).

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-Device:** Primary (vehicle-mounted components).
    - **Edge Computing:** Potential for offloading processing to edge servers (e.g., cloudlets).
    - **Cloud-Based:** For reporting, analytics, or visualization (e.g., ACC services).

  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - **On-Device:** High reliability via redundant sensors (e.g., dual LiDAR) and fault-tolerant ROS2.
    - **Cloud/Edge:** Reliability depends on network uptime (Wi-Fi) and cloud provider SLAs.
    - **Not in Document:** Specific uptime guarantees (e.g., 99.9%) or redundancy strategies for critical components (e.g., motor/steering control).
  "
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for **Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS)**—specifically in the context of **adaptive cruise control (ACC)** and **vehicle-to-everything (V2X) services**—must adhere to strict **data governance frameworks** as defined in **ISO 15622** and **3GPP TS 22.185**. Data ownership is implicitly governed by the **service requirements for V2X communications**, where **data sharing agreements** are standardized through **security-enforced communication protocols** (e.g., **5G-based V2X services**) to ensure compliance with **data protection regulations** such as GDPR or regional transport-specific standards.

**Privacy protection measures** are implicitly addressed in **ISO 26262-1 (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles)**, where **data anonymization** is critical for safety-critical systems to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive vehicle telemetry (e.g., speed, braking events). Consent management is implied through **secure authentication mechanisms** (e.g., **X.509 certificates** or **ECDSA signatures**) in **3GPP’s V2X service specifications**, ensuring only authorized entities (e.g., fleet operators, emergency services) can access Twin data.

No explicit mention of **consent management systems** or **data anonymization algorithms** is provided in the documents, but the **ISO 15622** standard requires **real-time data validation** for ACC systems, where **safety-critical data** (e.g., sensor inputs) must be processed with **confidentiality guarantees** via **end-to-end encryption (E2EE)** in V2X communications.

For **data sharing agreements**, the **3GPP TS 22.185** specifies **service-level agreements (SLAs)** for V2X data exchange, where **latency constraints** (e.g., <100ms for collision avoidance) must align with **privacy-preserving protocols** (e.g., **homomorphic encryption**) to balance real-time performance with compliance.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard is the **core compliance framework** for this Digital Twin system, with the following specifications:

1. **Industry Standards**:
   - **ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing)**:
     - **Part 2**: Defines **entity-based reference architecture (RA)** with OMEs, Data Collection Entities, DT Entities, and User Entities.
     - **Part 4**: Governs **information exchange** (e.g., OPC UA for data interoperability).

2. **Technologies & Protocols**:
   - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: Mandated for **data exchange** between DTs and OMEs (e.g., sensor-to-DT communication).
   - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Implemented via **modular, standards-aligned implementations** (e.g., OPC UA-compliant FE code).

3. **Certification & Compliance**:
   - **Framework**: Adherence to **ISO 23247** ensures interoperability and fidelity.
   - **Interoperability**: Enforced via **OPC UA** for cross-platform DT-OME communication.

*Note*: The documents reference a **public demonstration** (e.g., [GitHub Automotive-DT](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin)) but do not specify certification bodies or additional standards beyond ISO 23247.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for **ITS applications** (e.g., adaptive cruise control, V2X communications) incorporates **cybersecurity protocols** and **safety-critical mechanisms** as defined in **ISO 15622** and **ISO 26262-1**.

**Cybersecurity protocols** include:
- **Authentication**: **3GPP TS 22.185** mandates **mutual authentication** between vehicles and infrastructure via **X.509 certificates** or **short-term cryptographic keys** (e.g., **ECDH**) for V2X communications.
- **Access control**: **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** is implied in **ISO 15622**, where only **licensed entities** (e.g., OEMs, fleet managers) can interact with the Twin’s **safety-critical data** (e.g., sensor fusion outputs).
- **Threat mitigation**: **ISO 26262-1** requires **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., **redundant sensor validation**) to prevent cyberattacks (e.g., **man-in-the-middle**) on ACC systems, with **real-time intrusion detection** (e.g., **anomaly-based monitoring**) enforced via **ISO/IEC 27034-3** principles (not explicitly cited but implied).
- **Fail-safe mechanisms**: **ISO 15622** specifies **hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing** for ACC systems, where **safety-isolated components** (e.g., **ASIL-D** in ISO 26262) ensure **zero-downtime failures** during Twin execution.

**Safety-critical considerations** include:
- **Risk assessment**: **ISO 26262-1** mandates **hazard analysis** (e.g., **HAZOP**) for Twin data streams, where **criticality levels** (ASIL A-D) dictate **real-time processing** (e.g., **100Hz sampling rate** for ACC).
- **Safety validation**: **Model-driven architecture (MDA)** for **interactive digital twin cockpits** (Dalibor et al., 2020) ensures **safety-critical logic** is **formally verified** via **automated theorem provers** (e.g., **Coq, Isabelle**) to prevent **logical inconsistencies** in Twin simulations.
- **Physical-cyber-physical (PCP) synchronization**: **ISO 15622** requires **time-synchronized Twin updates** (e.g., **PTP protocol**) to align **digital and physical states** for **real-time collision avoidance**.

No explicit mention of **quantum-resistant cryptography** or **post-quantum security** is provided, but **ISO 26262-1** implies future-proofing via **standardized risk mitigation** (e.g., **ASIL re-evaluation**).
"
]
